acceptance_criteria: Approving a Work Graph writes an attributed, content-hashed approval record into a git-tracked file in the Target Repository alongside the existing Agentflow Home evidence, following the append-only evidence conventions
Approval currency for the exact graph hash can be verified from the repository alone, on a machine or CI runner with no Agentflow Home state
Existing home-directory approval evidence and the capture gate keep working unchanged
summary: Mirror Work Graph approvals into the Target Repository so approval currency is verifiable on any machine

acceptance_criteria: The command exits zero only when every .agentflow/work/ graph file validates and the repo-tracked approval is current for the exact graph content hash
Invalid graph, missing approval, and stale approval each fail with a distinct, actionable message
The command needs no network and no Agentflow Home state, so it runs identically in CI and on any developer machine
summary: agentflow work verify: a deterministic, CI-runnable graph and approval health check

acceptance_criteria: Files under .agentflow/proposals/ carry a schema-validated shape (kind new-work or completion-claim, summary, acceptance criteria, optional relates_to) and writing them never invalidates the approved graph hash or blocks any commit

Deterministic ingest applies valid new-work proposals as status proposed Work Items with content-derived ids, capped and deduped against the graph and each other; invalid proposals are reported, never applied and never fatal

Completion-claim proposals never close a Work Item by themselves; they surface for human disposition, and no ingest path lets a proposal become ready work without explicit human approval of the updated graph
summary: A quarantined proposals inbox any agent may write, ingested into the graph only through deterministic validation

acceptance_criteria: A deterministic generator renders open and proposed Work Items with their acceptance criteria plus the claim-and-propose protocol into WORK.md, clearly marked as generated with a pointer to the authoritative graph files
Every graph-mutating Agentflow command regenerates the mirror, and regeneration is idempotent so an up-to-date mirror produces no diff
summary: A generated WORK.md board that makes the Work Graph visible to any agent's normal context gathering

acceptance_criteria: init writes an always-on conventions section to the target's agent instructions: consult WORK.md before starting, carry Work-Item ids in commits for matching open items, write a proposal first for uncovered work, and never edit .agentflow/work/ directly

Vendored, dependency-free hooks run offline in milliseconds: pre-commit refuses staged .agentflow/work/ changes while always allowing .agentflow/proposals/, and commit-msg suggests a Work-Item trailer in observe mode and requires one only in strict mode

The enforcement mode defaults to observe, is recorded as committed repository policy, and in observe mode a developer with no Agentflow knowledge is never blocked by anything except direct .agentflow/work/ edits
summary: agentflow init installs passive agent conventions and self-contained observe/strict git hooks

acceptance_criteria: The report deterministically lists commits since the last approval with no Work-Item trailer, trailers naming unknown or closed items, and commits touching the declared file scopes of open items without carrying that item's trailer
The command mutates nothing, needs no Agentflow Home state, and exits nonzero only in strict mode when blocking findings exist so observe-mode CI stays green
summary: agentflow work drift: a read-only reconciliation report of work landed outside the graph

acceptance_criteria: reconcile walks drift findings and completion-claim proposals into proposed dispositions per affected Work Item: still valid, completed externally, partially done with amended acceptance criteria, or invalidated back to Framing
A completed-externally disposition records attributed external-completion evidence naming the outside commits, explicitly distinct from Run-backed completion, and never fabricates Run evidence
No disposition is applied without explicit human confirmation, and one confirmation pass yields a single updated graph ready for one re-approval
summary: agentflow work reconcile: disposition-based recovery that absorbs external work in one human review
